sheaf on the site of Cartesian spaces.
    -- This involves showing that for any open cover, the sections of the sheaf satisfy the descent condition.
    -- Specifically, we need to show that the gluing property holds for D-smooth maps from Euclidean spaces to the space of alternating multilinear maps.
    -- This is a straightforward application of the fact that the space of alternating multilinear maps is a vector space and the topology on it is the usual topology.
    -- We can use the fact that the derivative of a smooth map is smooth and the composition of smooth maps is smooth to establish the necessary properties.
    rw [CartSp.openCoverTopology]
    -- Use the general fact that the space of smooth maps from a manifold to a vector space is a sheaf.
    refine' isSheaf_of_hasProdAndSimplicial _ _ _
    <;> simp_all [hasProd_Ici]
    <;> try infer_instance
    <;> try
      {
        apply DSmoothMap.hasProd
        <;> infer_instance
      }
    <;> try
      {
        apply DSmoothMap.simplicial
        <;> infer_instance
      }
    <;> try
      {
        apply DSmoothMap.diagonal
        <;> infer_instance
      }
    <;> try
      {
        apply DSmoothMap.poddiagonal
        <;> infer_instance
      }
